userStories.title: Prisma TicketLink model + provider detection utility
userStories.description: Add the `TicketLink` Prisma model to `schema.prisma` with fields `id`, `ticketId`, `url`, `provider`, `externalRef`, `createdAt`, a `@@unique([ticketId, url])` constraint, and `onDelete: Cascade` relation to `Ticket`. Add `links TicketLink[]` to the `Ticket` model. Create the `detectProvider(url: string): ParsedLink` utility that parses GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket PR/MR URLs into `{ provider, externalRef }` without throwing on malformed input.
acceptanceCriteria: When `detectProvider('https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/42')` is called, it returns `{ provider: 'github', externalRef: 'owner/repo#42' }`
When `detectProvider('https://gitlab.com/owner/repo/-/merge_requests/7')` is called, it returns `{ provider: 'gitlab', externalRef: 'owner/repo#7' }`
When `detectProvider('https://bitbucket.org/owner/repo/pull-requests/3')` is called, it returns `{ provider: 'bitbucket', externalRef: 'owner/repo#3' }`
When `detectProvider('https://example.com/anything')` is called, it returns `{ provider: 'other', externalRef: null }`
When `detectProvider('not-a-url')` is called with a malformed URL, it returns `{ provider: 'other', externalRef: null }` without throwing
When `detectProvider('')` is called with an empty string, it returns `{ provider: 'other', externalRef: null }` without throwing
Given the Prisma schema is migrated, when a `TicketLink` row is inserted with a duplicate `(ticketId, url)` pair, then Prisma throws a unique constraint violation error

userStories.title: TicketLinks API ŌĆö POST, GET, DELETE endpoints
userStories.description: Create the `TicketLinksModule` with controller and service implementing three endpoints nested under `/projects/:projectSlug/tickets/:ref/links`. `POST` creates a link (deduplicates: returns `200` + existing when URL already linked, otherwise `201` + new). `GET` returns all links as `TicketLinkResponseDto[]`. `DELETE /:linkId` removes a link by id (404 if not found on that ticket). All endpoints use `@Public()` + JWT/API-key guard consistent with the tickets controller. Provider and externalRef are auto-populated via `detectProvider` on create.
acceptanceCriteria: When `POST /projects/:slug/tickets/:ref/links` is called with `{ url: 'https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/1' }` and the ticket exists, it returns HTTP 201 with a `TicketLinkResponseDto` where `provider` is `'github'` and `externalRef` is `'owner/repo#1'`
When `POST /projects/:slug/tickets/:ref/links` is called with a URL that is already linked to that ticket, it returns HTTP 200 and the existing `TicketLinkResponseDto` without creating a duplicate
When `POST /projects/:slug/tickets/:ref/links` is called with `{ url: 'not-a-url' }`, it returns HTTP 400
When `POST /projects/:slug/tickets/:ref/links` is called with a non-existent ticket ref, it returns HTTP 404
When `GET /projects/:slug/tickets/:ref/links` is called for a ticket with two links, it returns HTTP 200 with an array of two `TicketLinkResponseDto` objects
When `GET /projects/:slug/tickets/:ref/links` is called for a ticket with no links, it returns HTTP 200 with an empty array
When `DELETE /projects/:slug/tickets/:ref/links/:linkId` is called with a valid linkId that belongs to that ticket, it returns HTTP 204 with no body
When `DELETE /projects/:slug/tickets/:ref/links/:linkId` is called with a linkId that does not exist on that ticket, it returns HTTP 404

userStories.title: Include links[] in ticket show response
userStories.description: Extend `TicketResponseDto` with a `links: TicketLinkResponseDto[]` field. Update `TicketsService.findByRef()` (and any other place that assembles a full ticket response) to include the `links` relation via Prisma `include`. The field must always be present as an array ŌĆö never `undefined` or `null`. Run `bun run generate` after this change to regenerate CLI/web clients.
acceptanceCriteria: When `GET /projects/:slug/tickets/:ref` is called for a ticket with no links, the response body includes `"links": []`
When `GET /projects/:slug/tickets/:ref` is called after a link has been created via `POST .../links`, the response `links` array contains that `TicketLinkResponseDto`
When `GET /projects/:slug/tickets/:ref` is called, each item in `links` has the fields `id`, `url`, `provider`, `externalRef`, and `createdAt`

userStories.title: CLI ticket link and ticket unlink commands
userStories.description: Add `ticket link <ref> --url <url>` and `ticket unlink <ref> --url <url>` subcommands to `apps/cli/src/commands/ticket.ts`. `link` calls `POST .../links` and prints the created/existing `TicketLinkResponseDto`; with `--json` flag it outputs raw JSON. `unlink` fetches `GET .../links`, finds the entry matching the given URL, calls `DELETE .../links/:id`, and exits 0; if no matching URL is found it prints `'No link found for <url>'` and exits 1. Both commands resolve the project slug from config or ticket ref. Also verify that `koda ticket show <ref> --json` output includes the `links` array (no code change needed if regenerated client already includes it ŌĆö confirm and document).
acceptanceCriteria: When `koda ticket link KDA-42 --url https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/1` is executed, it calls `POST /projects/:slug/tickets/KDA-42/links` with `{ url }` and prints the provider and externalRef to stdout
When `koda ticket link KDA-42 --url https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/1 --json` is executed, it prints the raw `TicketLinkResponseDto` as JSON to stdout
When `koda ticket link KDA-42 --url <already-linked-url>` is executed, it prints the existing link without error and exits 0
When `koda ticket unlink KDA-42 --url https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/1` is executed and a matching link exists, it calls `DELETE .../links/:id` and exits with code 0

When `koda ticket unlink KDA-42 --url https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/1` is executed and no matching link exists, it prints `'No link found for https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/1'` and exits with code 1
When `koda ticket show KDA-42 --json` is executed for a ticket with linked PRs, the JSON output includes a `"links"` array with at least one entry containing `"provider"` and `"url"` fields

analysis:
## Codebase Analysis

### Architecture Overview
Koda is a Turborepo monorepo with NestJS 11 + Fastify API (`apps/api`), Nuxt 3 web (`apps/web`), and Commander.js CLI (`apps/cli`). The API uses Nathapp conventions: `AppFactory` bootstrap, `JsonResponse.Ok<T>()` for responses, `AppException` for errors, `@Public()` / `@Principal()` decorators for auth.

### Existing Patterns to Follow

**Prisma Schema** (`apps/api/prisma/schema.prisma`):
- All models use `@id @default(cuid())` for primary keys
- Relations use `onDelete: Cascade` for child records (e.g. TicketLabel, TicketActivity)
- No Prisma enums ŌĆö local TypeScript enums in `src/common/enums.ts` (SQLite limitation)
- Ticket model has `links TicketLink[]` relation to add

**Nested Resource Pattern** (`apps/api/src/comments/`):
- `CommentsController` is decorated with `@Controller('projects/:projectSlug/tickets/:ref/comments')`
- Service resolves ticket via `findByRef()` from TicketsService
- `CommentsService` injected into controller alongside `TicketsService`
- Response DTOs are plain classes with `@ApiProperty()` decorators
- `JsonResponse.Ok()` wraps all 200/201 responses; 204 returns void

**Ticket Resolution**: `TicketsService.findByRef(projectSlug, ref)` resolves both CUID and human-readable refs (KDA-42) ŌĆö use this, do not re-implement.

**TicketResponseDto**: Currently assembled in `tickets.service.ts` ŌĆö needs `links: TicketLinkResponseDto[]` added. Must include `links` in all places where ticket is returned (findByRef, findAll if needed, etc.).

**CLI Pattern** (`apps/cli/src/commands/ticket.ts`):
- All subcommands use generated service clients from `apps/cli/src/generated/`
- `--json` flag outputs raw JSON via `console.log(JSON.stringify(result, null, 2))`
- Non-JSON output uses formatted console.log with labels
- Exit codes: 0 = success, 1 = not found / logical error, 2 = API error
- Config resolved via `getConfig()` from `apps/cli/src/config.ts`
- After API changes: `bun run generate` regenerates `apps/cli/src/generated/` ŌĆö CLI commands should use the generated `TicketLinksService`

### Files to Modify
1. `apps/api/prisma/schema.prisma` ŌĆö add `TicketLink` model and `links` relation on `Ticket`
2. `apps/api/src/tickets/tickets.controller.ts` ŌĆö add 3 nested link endpoints
3. `apps/api/src/tickets/tickets.service.ts` ŌĆö include `links` in ticket response
4. `apps/cli/src/commands/ticket.ts` ŌĆö add `link` and `unlink` subcommands

### New Files to Create
1. `apps/api/src/ticket-links/ticket-links.module.ts`
2. `apps/api/src/ticket-links/ticket-links.controller.ts`
3. `apps/api/src/ticket-links/ticket-links.service.ts`
4. `apps/api/src/ticket-links/dto/create-ticket-link.dto.ts`
5. `apps/api/src/ticket-links/dto/ticket-link-response.dto.ts`
6. `apps/api/src/ticket-links/utils/detect-provider.ts`
7. Migration file (auto-generated via `prisma migrate dev`)

### Risks
- `TicketResponseDto` is used in many endpoints ŌĆö adding `links[]` must be backward-compatible (empty array, never undefined)
- `detectProvider` must not throw on malformed/non-URL input
- Deduplication on `(ticketId, url)` unique constraint: `POST` on duplicate must return `200` + existing record, not `201` or an error ŌĆö requires try/catch on unique constraint violation or upsert logic
- CLI `unlink` has no linkId ŌĆö must fetch links list first, find by URL, then DELETE by id
- After API changes, `bun run generate` must be run before CLI code references generated types

### Migration Notes
- Run `cd apps/api && bun run prisma migrate dev --name add-ticket-links` after schema change
- Run `bun run generate` from monorepo root after API endpoints are added to regenerate CLI/web clients
